phase3_findings.description: docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md still asserts the retired rule ('they do not replace the complete pre-commit or CI run') sixteen lines below the new policy it contradicts — a stated AC violation and a self-contradicting diff

The #707 retired-convention sweep hand-duplicates its five-literal list between the sweep and its positive control, and the control plants only one literal while its comment claims a mistype of any arm goes RED

The module header and the inventory both attribute assert_eq to module-harness.sh and claim the module references NO monolith run.sh helper; assert_eq is defined by the caller (run.sh:251 / run-module.sh:323)
The inventory's exclusion table declares a population ('every Python entry point run.sh still drives') that its cloud_writer_contract.py row falsifies in both the membership and disposition claims
docs/cutovers/issue-600-audit-prompt-renderer.md still says the renderer tests are 'wired into lib/test/run.sh' — the stale half of a coupled mirror this extraction updated everywhere else
Both #530 ceilings were renegotiated without the in-doc decision-record note the budget doc's own #621/#655 precedent requires, so the provenance chain stops at #655

The new reflection obligation contradicts implement.md's own reflection-routing rule ('frictionless end-to-end → do not file a bullet') and its cheap-gate cost model, and is stated outside the guidance section the AC names

The parallelized final gate instructs the run to START the local full suite but never to READ its result, and states no failure arm — so a RED suite, a nonempty skip tally, or a run that never started are all invisible behind a completion claim. An authoritative signal nobody reads is not authoritative.
CONTRIBUTING.md carries the focused-default policy but is outside the retired-convention sweep's file set, so a revert of that file to the retired rule passes the whole suite
The run.sh placement comment claims #707's per-file pins all join the #563 loop; the two final-gate pins directly above it cannot (their operative sentences differ per file)
The inventory's shared-label routing caveat presents #600 and #707 as the only split-label cases; #591 is a third (module carries the ratchet driver, run.sh keeps its presence pins)
One of five fixture allocations in the new module drops the fail-closed mktemp guard its four siblings carry, misattributing a scratch-exhaustion RED to the coverage-map guard
The #600 and #668 blocks keep the bare redirect + positional $? capture idiom that this same file's #527 comment declares superseded, and the #707 control reads $? positionally

notes: Phase 2 was resolved orchestrator-direct (lite path) by running each mechanically-decidable checklist item as a real command — the focused module, test_module_runner.py, the coverage-map/census/gh-api-path guards, the retired-literal sweep, and the pin-literal resolution — rather than dispatching verifier subagents. Verifier dispatch was reserved for what execution could not settle.

Verification tier: focused. run-module.sh harness-python-guards 31/31; run-module.sh review-and-fix-contract 68/68; test_module_runner.py 61 OK; regenerate-artifacts exit 0; shellcheck clean. The full lib/test/run.sh run started concurrently with the push to origin at the final gate, per the policy this PR ships.
